reason_phase_5_blocked: Current replay labels (storm_relative_top30_label) are storm-invariant: all 5 storm events share the identical label vector.
Storm-invariant labels reflect per-community historical outage aggregates, not storm-specific outage behavior.
Bayesian optimization against storm-invariant labels would optimize toward a static historical baseline, not cross-storm generalization.
Labels share source lineage with historical_prior feature (both derive from n_outage_events). Optimizing would amplify same-source bias.
Phase 5 constrained Bayesian optimization is blocked until storm-variant labels or a second ground-truth source are available.
valid_evidence: Scenario sensitivity exists: rain +20% produces avg rank change 0.85 and top-3 stability 0.8667
Historical prior removal barely changes metrics (rank similarity 0.9786) — model not collapsed into historical outage ranking
Model produces explainable rankings driven by hazard × vulnerability factors
Model is stable across storm scenarios (LOSO rank stability = 0.9725)
All 75 replay rows produce valid risk scores in [0,100] range
Community hazard modifiers differentiate communities within storms (wind: 0.75–1.25, rain: 0.80–1.25, tide: 0.75–1.25)
Model supports 6 configurable weight profiles validated structurally (11/11 PASS)
Urban load proxy differentiates urban vs. rural communities (0.18–0.90 range)
diagnostic_only_evidence: Top-3 capture rate (0.00) — computed against storm-invariant label; reflects multi-factor triage divergence, not model failure
Top-5 capture rate (0.20) — computed against storm-invariant label; partial overlap with historical outage aggregates
NDCG@5 (0.14) — rank alignment against static labels; low due to labels being storm-invariant aggregates
Spearman rank correlation (-0.64) — negative correlation confirms systematic rank divergence between triage priority and historical outage ranking
Separation score (-0.42) — model top-3 prioritize tree/vulnerability factors over outage history
LOSO metrics — non-informative because labels are storm-invariant; measures formula stability across scenarios, not predictive generalization
Ablation component importance — relative ranking of component impact; reflects current weight configuration, not ground truth
Risk calibration bins — score band diagnostics, not probability calibration
known_limitations: Communities identified by centroid proxy, not official HRM polygon boundaries
Vulnerability features include seed proxy values, not measured observations
Storm replay scenario parameters are hypothetical, not meteorological forecasts
Top-K validation metrics are diagnostic replay evidence, not proof of future outage prediction accuracy

Tide sensitivity is zero because storm-level perturbation is uniform — all community tide scores scale proportionally, preserving rank order. tide_exposure_modifiers do vary (0.75–1.25) but proportional scaling is a mathematical property of the multiplier design. Storm-variant surge exposure data needed for non-zero tide sensitivity.
Risk scores are triage priority rankings normalized to [0,100]. They are not probabilities, not calibrated probabilities, and cannot be interpreted as predicted outage rate.
Phase 4.5 validation reframes metrics as diagnostic evidence. Model is validated as a scenario-driven triage ranking prototype, not as a future-outcome forecast system.
future_data_needed: Storm-specific outage counts (per community, per storm event) — to enable storm-variant labels and unblock Phase 5
NS Power spatial outage data (aggregated by community polygon) — independent ground-truth source
Public_Trees WIRES field (tree-wire proximity category) — physically-causal wind exposure feature to replace seed proxy tree_exposure
Street_Lights density (per-community count) — grid density proxy to replace seed rural_line_proxy
CivicAddresses with GSA_NAME (per-community count) — population density proxy to replace seed urban_load_proxy
HRM official boundary polygons — true polygon-level spatial joins replacing centroid proxies
Flood extent polygons from files.halifax.ca — ground-truth flood risk zones replacing seed lowland_exposure
demo_safe_claims: Model is explainable — every risk score decomposes into hazard, vulnerability, prior, and interaction terms
Model uses configurable weights (6 profiles available)
Model uses community hazard modifiers (wind/rain/tide per community)
Model includes urban load proxy for grid density differentiation
Model did not collapse into historical outage ranking — removing prior barely changes ranking
Scenario sensitivity exists — especially rain +20% (rank change 0.85)
Known limitations are clearly documented across all outputs
Output is response priority, not outage probability
demo_unsafe_claims: Outage prediction accuracy — not validated; labels are storm-invariant and same-source
Probabilistic calibration (ROC AUC, Brier score) — risk scores are not probabilities
Bayesian optimization results — Phase 5 is blocked
PCA, feature space analysis — structural diagnostics only, not demo material
Label leakage quantification — diagnostic analysis, not demo content
Same-source bias analysis — diagnostic analysis, not demo content
Cross-storm generalization — LOSO not informative, labels are storm-invariant
